title: Consent Record (PHI-free, hash-chained consent event — LIVE_PLAN L12 / R-40)
description: One append-only consent EVENT in the consent store (verification/consent-store.js). Consent capture is a RECORDING mechanism, not a permission unlock: persistContent() stays synthetic-only and session-store still destroys on close. Records are PHI-FREE BY CONSTRUCTION: an encounter-scoped session_ref (never demographics/IHI — Trust Boundary 4), enums, omnibus path bindings, and hashes only; additionalProperties:false makes a free-text field unrepresentable. The record's current status is derived from the LATEST event per (session_ref, consent_type). Omnibus-sourced consent types carry a proven omnibus binding (provenPath + the pinned dataset receipt) — a consent type is never minted. entry_hash = sha256(canonical(entry-without-entry_hash) + prev_hash), same chain algorithm as the audit ledger; entry_hash IS the plan's record_sha256. Zod mirror: verification/consent-schema.js (this JSON schema is the source of truth).

session_ref.description: Encounter-scoped reference only (session-store ref). NEVER demographics, IHI, or any identity artefact — identity stays inside the identity-au boundary.

consent_type.description: v1 scope per the approved CONSENT-PLAN: session_persistence (first-party) + mhr_data_sharing / telehealth_consent (omnibus-bound, RECORD-ONLY — nothing acts on MHR).

omnibus_binding.description: Proof for omnibus-sourced types: the proven omnibus path + the pinned dataset receipt fields. null for first-party types. A consent type is never minted.

status.description: FHIR-aligned (omnibus Consent status subset). granted→active; declined→rejected; revoked/session-end→inactive.

reason.description: Why this event was recorded — enum only, never narrative.

provision_actions.description: Omnibus provision_actions subset the consent covers.

policy_rule.description: Capture is always opt-in (omnibus policyRule code).

expires.description: Session-bound by default — no standing consent in v1; closeEncounter() inactivates mechanically.

mode.description: Normalised via verification/mode.js — mock is never presented as live.
